task_completion.description: Create the base rectangular plate in FreeCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeCAD interface is open with a 3D rectangular plate visible in the viewport or tree view.

task_completion.description: Set the plate dimensions to 250mm x 200mm x 12mm
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sketcher constraints, pad properties, or spreadsheet values showing 250mm (width), 200mm (height), and 12mm (thickness).

task_completion.description: Create two 2x2 bolt hole groups (8 holes total)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D model shows exactly 8 holes. Four holes are in a 2x2 pattern in the upper-left quadrant, and four holes are in a 2x2 pattern along the left edge.

task_completion.description: Apply a 45-degree, 6mm depth chamfer along the top edge
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top edge of the 3D plate has a visible angled cut (chamfer), and/or the tree view shows a Chamfer feature with a 6mm parameter.

task_completion.description: Create a spreadsheet within the model listing the required parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: A FreeCAD Spreadsheet view is open showing cells with text labels for plate width, height, thickness, bolt diameter, and gauge, along with their corresponding values.

task_completion.description: Save as FCStd and export as STEP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or FreeCAD save dialog shows 'gusset_plate.FCStd' and 'gusset_plate.step' located in the /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeCAD GUI to design the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD graphical interface with toolbars, tree view, and 3D viewport being actively used, rather than just a terminal running a python script.
The spreadsheet is visible as a tab or window *within* the FreeCAD application, not in an external program like LibreOffice Calc.
The viewport displays a shaded 3D solid model of the plate with holes and chamfers, not just a 2D sketch or text description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The gusset plate must be exactly 250mm wide x 200mm tall x 12mm thick. There must be exactly 8 bolt holes (22mm clearance diameter) arranged in two 2x2 groups (one upper-left, one along the left edge). The top edge must have a 45-degree, 6mm depth chamfer. The output files must be saved as 'gusset_plate.FCStd' and 'gusset_plate.step' in '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/'.
